title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: Live-blog coverage and summary of Fabio Giglietto’s presentation at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference in Cape Town. The write-up highlights Giglietto's research assessing how Facebook's changing algorithmic interventions over a six-year period impacted user engagement with partisan versus quality news URLs.
description: A live-blog coverage and summary of Fabio Giglietto’s presentation at the ICA 2026 conference, analyzing how Facebook's algorithmic tweaks impacted user engagement with news URLs.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: A news report details the societal and electoral implications of Facebook's feed customization. It quotes and references Fabio Giglietto's study on Meta's algorithmic policies, discussing how automated filters designed to limit 'civic content' inadvertently marginalized democratic political speech and turned fringe extremist networks into highly visible informational sources.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: News coverage of a major research study led by Fabio Giglietto showing how Facebook's automated efforts to downrank political content severely impacted institutional politicians (a 43% to 73% drop in reach) while allowing anti-establishment and conspiracy-related content to thrive. The article describes how this research prompted Italian lawmakers to discuss bipartisan regulatory measures.
description: An Italian news article reporting on a University of Urbino study led by Fabio Giglietto, which reveals how Facebook's algorithm penalizes mainstream political content while boosting extremist reach.
